audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for greatness, child/little ones, stumbling-blocks, lost sheep, community restoration process, binding/loosing, agreement prayer, seventy-seven forgiveness, and unforgiving servant parable.

verses.restored: At that hour the disciples came to Jesus, saying, Who then is greatest in the reign of the heavens?
Calling a child, he stood the child in their midst
and said, Amen, I say to you: unless you turn and become like children, you will certainly not enter the reign of the heavens.
Therefore whoever humbles himself like this child, this one is greatest in the reign of the heavens.
Whoever receives one such child in my name receives me.
But whoever causes one of these little ones trusting in me to stumble, it would be better for him that a large millstone be hung around his neck and he be sunk in the depth of the sea.
Woe to the world because of stumbling-blocks; for stumbling-blocks must come, yet woe to the person through whom the stumbling-block comes.
If your hand or foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it from you; it is better to enter life maimed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the age-long fire.
If your eye causes you to stumble, remove it and throw it from you; it is better to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the Gehenna of fire.
See that you do not despise one of these little ones; for I say to you that their messengers in the heavens continually see the face of my Father in the heavens.
[Some later manuscripts add: For the Son of Humanity came to save the lost.]
What do you think? If a person has a hundred sheep and one of them wanders, does he not leave the ninety-nine on the mountains and go seek the wandering one?
If he finds it, amen, I say to you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine that did not wander.
In the same way, it is not the will before your Father in the heavens that one of these little ones should be lost.
If your brother or sister sins/misaligns, go and show the fault between you and that one alone; if that one hears you, you have gained your brother or sister.
But if that one does not hear, take with you one or two more, so that by the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
If that one refuses to hear them, tell it to the assembly; and if that one refuses to hear even the assembly, let that one be to you as the Gentile and the tax collector.
Amen, I say to you: whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will have been loosed in heaven.
Again, amen, I say to you: if two of you agree on earth about any matter they ask, it will come to be for them from my Father in the heavens.
For where two or three are gathered into my name, there I am in their midst.
Then Peter came and said to him, Lord, how often will my brother or sister sin/misalign against me and I release them? Up to seven times?
Jesus said to him, I do not say to you up to seven times, but up to seventy-seven times.
Because of this, the reign of the heavens is like a human king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
As he began to settle, one debtor of ten thousand talents was brought to him.
Since he did not have enough to repay, the lord commanded him to be sold, with wife and children and all he had, and repayment to be made.
The servant fell down and bowed before him, saying, Be patient with me, and I will repay you everything.
Moved with compassion, the lord of that servant released him and forgave/released the debt.
But that servant went out and found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii; seizing him, he began to choke him, saying, Repay what you owe.
His fellow servant fell down and begged him, saying, Be patient with me, and I will repay you.
But he was not willing; instead he went and threw him into prison until he should repay the debt.
When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were deeply grieved and came and explained to their lord all that had happened.
Then his lord called him and said, Harmful servant, I forgave/released you all that debt because you begged me.
Was it not necessary for you also to have mercy on your fellow servant, as I had mercy on you?
In anger his lord handed him to the jailers until he should repay all that was owed.
So also my heavenly Father will do to you if each of you does not release your brother or sister from your hearts.
